name: test-fixing
description: Run tests and systematically fix all failing tests using smart error grouping. Use when user asks to fix failing tests, mentions test failures, runs test suite and failures occur, or requests to make tests pass.
qualityReason: Actionable, systematic approach to test repair
capabilities: Run test suite and identify all failing tests with error analysis
Group similar test failures by error type, affected module, and root cause
Prioritize fixing by impact - infrastructure errors first, then logic issues
Verify each fix by running focused test subsets before moving to next group
Run complete test suite to ensure no regressions after all fixes
Follow systematic approach: identify, group, fix, verify, repeat
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) with no executable code. The detected 'external_commands' are markdown examples of CLI commands, not actual shell executions. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' finding is a scanner misidentification with no cryptographic code present.
valueStatement: When tests fail after refactoring or code changes, this skill groups errors by type and root cause, then fixes each group methodically to get the test suite passing.
limitations: Skill does not execute code - it provides methodology and guidance only
User must run actual test commands (make test, pytest) themselves
Does not fix flaky tests or timing-dependent test failures
Cannot automatically understand complex business logic bugs

useCases.targetUser: Developer who completed a refactor and now has failing tests
useCases.title: Fix Tests After Refactoring
useCases.description: After renaming modules or functions across a codebase, this skill helps identify all affected test failures, groups them by type, and systematically fixes each group.

useCases.targetUser: Developer debugging CI/test failures in pull requests
useCases.title: Handle CI/CD Test Failures
useCases.description: When CI pipeline fails due to test errors, use this skill to group failures, understand root causes, and fix systematically before retrying the pipeline.

useCases.targetUser: Developer working on legacy codebase with accumulated test issues
useCases.title: Debug Multiple Test Failures
useCases.description: When running a large test suite reveals many failures, this skill helps prioritize which to fix first based on dependency order and impact.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Test Fix Request
promptTemplates.prompt: The tests are failing after my recent changes. Please help me fix them using the test-fixing skill. Run the test suite first to see all failures.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to fix failing tests and needs systematic approach

promptTemplates.title: Post-Refactor Test Fixing
promptTemplates.description: After refactoring, many tests fail with different error types. Group them logically and fix systematically.
promptTemplates.prompt: I refactored my codebase and now 15 tests are failing. Use smart error grouping to categorize these failures and fix them in the right order. Start by running make test to see all failures.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has many test failures after code refactoring

promptTemplates.title: CI Pipeline Test Debugging
promptTemplates.description: Focus on infrastructure and configuration errors first, then move to logic issues.
promptTemplates.prompt: My CI pipeline failed due to test errors. Use the test-fixing skill to identify the root cause of each failure. Prioritize infrastructure issues like import errors and missing dependencies first.

promptTemplates.title: Subset Test Fixing
promptTemplates.description: Fix a specific group of related test failures in isolation.
promptTemplates.prompt: I have 5 ImportError failures in my auth module tests. Fix these specific failures first, verify they pass, then we will move to other error groups.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to fix specific subset of test failures

bestPractices: Run focused test subsets after each fix to verify the change works before moving to the next group
Fix infrastructure issues (imports, dependencies, config) before logic bugs for cleaner error states
Use git diff to understand what changed recently and identify root causes of test failures
Keep changes minimal and focused - fix one issue at a time rather than bulk changes
antiPatterns: Fixing all tests at once without running subsets - this makes it hard to identify which fix resolved which error
Ignoring infrastructure errors and trying to fix logic errors first - import failures will mask real issues
Making large, sweeping changes instead of targeted fixes - increases risk of introducing new bugs
Skipping the final full test suite run - may miss regressions introduced during fixing

faq.question: How does this skill handle test failures?
faq.answer: This skill runs the test suite, groups failures by error type and root cause, then fixes each group systematically starting with highest impact.

faq.question: Does this skill execute tests automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides the methodology and commands to run. You execute the test commands (make test, pytest) as the user.

faq.question: What test frameworks does this work with?
faq.answer: The skill works with any test framework (pytest, Jest, RSpec, etc.) - it focuses on error grouping methodology, not specific framework syntax.

faq.question: Can this fix flaky tests?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on deterministic test failures. Flaky tests require different debugging approaches.

faq.question: How do I prioritize which failures to fix first?
faq.answer: Follow the fix order strategy: infrastructure first (imports, dependencies), then API changes (signatures, modules), then logic issues (assertions, bugs).

faq.question: What if tests fail due to missing dependencies?
faq.answer: Missing dependencies are infrastructure errors - fix these first as they often cause cascading failures in other test groups.
